name: Peanut Butter Divinity
recipeInstructions:
1. In a buttered 2-quart saucepan, mix together the sugar, corn syrup, and water.
2. Cook and stir over medium heat until the mixture boils.
3. Continue cooking without stirring to reach the hard-ball stage or until a candy thermometer registers 260°F.
4. Meanwhile, beat the egg whites with an electric mixer until stiff but not dry.
5. Gradually pour the hot syrup over the egg whites, beating constantly at high speed.
6. Add vanilla and beat until the candy holds its shape (about 4-5 minutes).
7. On a towel dusted generously with confectioner's sugar, quickly pat the candy to a 10x4 inches rectangle.
8. Dust lightly with more confectioner's sugar and roll the candy into a 16x6 inches rectangle.
9. Spread peanut butter over the candy, about 1/2 inch from one of the long sides.
10. Roll up from the opposite side, using the towel to help roll it.
11. Wrap in the towel and chill until firm for about 1 hour.
12. Cut into half crosswise, wrap in waxed paper.
13. Refrigerate. To serve, cut into slices slightly more than 1/2 inch thick.
Makes about 48 pieces.
